data.zh: 第70/175号决议，附件。

data.en: Disarmament Commission
data.zh: 裁军审议委员会

data.en: Substantive session of 2023
data.zh: 2023年实质性会议

data.en: New York, 3–21 April 2023
data.zh: 2023年4月3日至21日，纽约

data.en: Agenda item 4
data.zh: 议程项目4

data.en: Recommendations for achieving the objective of nuclear disarmament and non-proliferation of nuclear weapons
data.zh: 关于实现核裁军和不扩散核武器目标的建议

data.en: Working Paper submitted by the Chair of Working Group I
data.zh: 第一工作组主席提交的工作文件

data.en: I.
data.zh: 一.

data.en: Introduction
data.zh: 导言

data.en: The Disarmament Commission adopted its agenda for the substantive sessions of 2019, 2022 and 2023 at their respective organizational sessions and decided to allocate to its Working Group I agenda item 4, entitled “Recommendations for achieving the objective of nuclear disarmament and non-proliferation of nuclear weapons”.
data.zh: 裁军审议委员会在相关组织会议上通过了2019年、2022年和2023年实质性会议议程，并决定将题为“关于实现核裁军和不扩散核武器目标的建议”的议程项目4分配给第一工作组。

data.en: The Working Group met from 4 to 19 April 2018, from 6 to 21 April 2022 and from 5 to 20 April 2023 to deliberate on the issues related to the agenda item, in accordance with the mandate of the Commission.
data.zh: 工作组于2018年4月4日至19日、2022年4月6日至21日和2023年4月5日至20日举行了会议，按照委员会的任务授权审议了与该议程项目有关的问题。

data.en: The Disarmament Commission is meeting during challenging times.
data.zh: 裁军审议委员会的会议是在充满挑战的时期举行。

data.en: Although the end of the Cold War was heralded as a propitious development for the nuclear disarmament and non-proliferation regime, with progress having been made on nuclear non-proliferation and disarmament, in recent time, the international community has been unable to make further progress in achieving the goal of nuclear disarmament and non-proliferation of nuclear weapons.
data.zh: 尽管冷战结束标志着核裁军和不扩散制度的一个有利发展，在核不扩散和裁军方面已取得进展，但近期以来，国际社会却未能在实现核裁军和不扩散核武器的目标方面取得进一步进展。

data.en: The Working Group recalled paragraph 34 of the final document of the first special session of the General Assembly devoted to disarmament (1978), which states, in its relevant part, that disarmament, the relaxation of the international context, the peaceful settlement of disputes in accordance with the Charter of the United Nations and the strengthening of international peace and security are directly related to each other.
data.zh: 工作组回顾了1978年第一届专门讨论裁军问题的大会特别会议最后文件第34段，其中相关部分指出，裁军、缓和国际局势、根据《联合国宪章》和平解决争端以及加强国际和平与安全彼此直接相关。

data.en: Progress in any of those spheres has a beneficial effect on all of them; in turn, failure in one sphere has negative effects on others.
data.zh: 其中任何一个领域的进展都有益于所有这些领域，而一个领域的失败也会对其他领域产生负面影响。

data.en: The Working Group held extensive discussions and exchanged views on the agenda item over the three substantive sessions, working towards reaching a consensus on the below set of objectives, principles and recommendations towards achieving the objective of nuclear disarmament and non-proliferation of nuclear weapons.
data.zh: 工作组在三次实质性会议上就该议程项目进行了广泛的讨论并交换了意见，争取就以下一系列目标、原则和建议达成共识，以实现核裁军和不扩散核武器的目标。

data.en: II.
data.zh: 二.

data.en: Objective and principle
data.zh: 目标和原则

data.en: The principal goal of nuclear disarmament is to achieve a world without nuclear weapons by completely and globally eliminating nuclear weapons in a verifiable, irreversible, non-discriminatory and transparent manner at the earliest possible date, thus contributing to a safer world for all.
data.zh: 核裁军的主要目标是尽早以可核查、不可逆、非歧视和透明的方式在全球彻底消除核武器，因此实现一个没有核武器的世界，从而为人人享有更安全的世界作出贡献。

data.en: The goal of non-proliferation of nuclear weapons is to prevent the spread of nuclear weapons and a nuclear arms race, including through the implementation of relevant multilateral, regional and national legal and political instruments, as well as prohibitions, in a verifiable, non-discriminatory and transparent manner, taking into account the inalienable right to nuclear energy for peaceful purposes.
data.zh: 不扩散核武器的目标是防止核武器扩散和核军备竞赛，包括以可核查、非歧视和透明的方式执行相关的多边、区域和国家法律文书、政治文书和禁令，同时考虑到和平利用核能的不可剥夺权利。

data.en: Nuclear disarmament and the non-proliferation of nuclear weapons are essential for strengthening international peace and security and for preventing a nuclear war and any use, intended or unintended, of nuclear weapons.
data.zh: 核裁军和不扩散核武器对于加强国际和平与安全、防止核战争及有意或无意使用核武器至关重要。

data.en: Nuclear disarmament and nuclear non-proliferation are mutually reinforcing processes, when pursued constructively and in good faith.
data.zh: 如果以建设性和真诚的方式推进，核裁军和核不扩散是相辅相成的进程。

data.en: Nuclear disarmament promotes international peace, security and stability on the basis of undiminished security for all.
data.zh: 核裁军在各国安全不受减损的基础上促进国际和平、安全和稳定。

data.en: All States share concerns related to the threat of terrorism and the risk that non‑State actors might acquire nuclear weapons and their means of delivery.
data.zh: 所有国家都对恐怖主义的威胁和非国家行为体可能获取核武器及其运载工具的风险感到关切。

data.en: We recall the obligation of all States to implement fully the relevant resolutions of the Security Council and of the General Assembly.
data.zh: 我们回顾，所有国家都有义务充分执行安全理事会和大会相关决议。

data.en: All States that have not yet done so are encouraged to become parties to the International Convention for the Suppression of Acts of Nuclear Terrorism as soon as possible.
data.zh: 鼓励所有尚未加入《制止核恐怖主义行为国际公约》的国家尽快加入该公约。

data.en: All States are aware of and concerned about the catastrophic humanitarian consequences of any use of nuclear weapons.
data.zh: 所有国家都意识到使用核武器的灾难性人道主义后果并表达了关切。

data.en: All States must comply, at all times, with their obligations under international law, including international humanitarian law.
data.zh: 所有国家在任何时候都必须遵守国际法包括国际人道法规定的义务。

data.en: The principles and purposes contained in the Charter of the United Nations, as well as the provisions of international humanitarian law – in particular the principle that the right of parties to an armed conflict to choose methods or means of warfare is not unlimited, the principles of distinction and proportionality, the prohibition against indiscriminate attacks, the prohibition on the use of weapons of a nature to cause superfluous injury or unnecessary suffering and the rules related to the protection of the environment – are fundamental to the pursuance of nuclear disarmament and the non-proliferation of nuclear weapons.
data.zh: 《联合国宪章》所载的原则和宗旨以及国际人道法的规定，特别是武装冲突各方都没有无限选择作战方法或手段的权利原则、区分原则和相称原则、禁止不分皂白的攻击、禁止使用造成过度伤害或不必要痛苦的武器，以及与保护环境有关的规则，对于实现核裁军和不扩散核武器至关重要。

data.en: While disarmament is the responsibility of all States, nuclear-weapon States have the primary responsibility for nuclear disarmament and, together with other militarily significant States, for halting and reversing the arms race.
data.zh: 裁军是所有国家的责任，但核武器国家对核裁军负有主要责任，并与其他军事大国一起，对停止和扭转军备竞赛负有主要责任。

data.en: All nuclear-weapon States reaffirm their unequivocal commitments and undertaking to accomplish the total elimination of their nuclear arsenals, leading to nuclear disarmament.
data.zh: 所有核武器国家都重申了对彻底消除核武库从而实现核裁军的明确承诺和保证。

data.en: All States not party to the Non-Proliferation Treaty which continue to rely on nuclear deterrence in their military and security doctrines also have a responsibility for nuclear disarmament and non-proliferation of nuclear weapons.
data.zh: 所有在军事和安全理论中继续依赖核威慑的非《不扩散条约》缔约国也对核裁军和不扩散核武器负有责任。

data.en: All States agree that the total elimination of nuclear weapons is the only absolute guarantee to eliminate the risk of use, or threat of use, of nuclear weapons.
data.zh: 所有国家都同意，彻底消除核武器是消除使用或威胁使用核武器风险的唯一绝对保证。

data.en: Nuclear risk reduction is an important but only provisional undertaking and is neither an alternative to or a substitute for nuclear disarmament, nor can it be implied or be interpreted as justifying the indefinite possession of nuclear weapons by any State.
data.zh: 减少核风险很重要，但只是一项临时性的工作，既不能替代核裁军，也不能被暗示或解释为任何国家可以无限期拥有核武器。

data.en: Pending the total elimination of nuclear weapons, nuclear risk reduction should be pursued concurrent with nuclear disarmament.
data.zh: 在彻底消除核武器之前，应在核裁军的同时减少核风险。

data.en: It can promote mutual trust among States through greater transparency, communication and confidence-building measures.
data.zh: 这有助于通过更广泛的透明度、交流和建立信任措施，增进国家间的相互信任。

data.en: Pending total elimination of nuclear weapons, strengthening negative security assurances would contribute to building confidence in the non-proliferation regime, progress in nuclear disarmament as well as improving the overall security environment.
data.zh: 在彻底消除核武器之前，加强消极安全保证将有助于建立对不扩散制度的信心、推动核裁军取得进展并改善总体安全环境。

data.en: In this context, United Nations Security Council resolution 984 (1995) noted the unilateral statements by each of the nuclear-weapon States, in which they gave conditional or unconditional security assurances against the use and the threat of use of nuclear weapons to non-nuclear-weapon States, and the relevant protocols to nuclear-weapon-free zone treaties and declarations by the nuclear-weapon States regarding Mongolia’s nuclear-weapon-free status, recognizing that treaty-based security assurances are available to such zones.
data.zh: 在这方面，联合国安全理事会第984(1995)号决议注意到每个核武器国家发表的单方面声明，其中作出了不对无核武器国家使用和威胁使用核武器的有条件或无条件的安全保证，还注意到无核武器区条约的相关议定书以及核武器国家关于蒙古无核武器地位的声明，承认无核武器区可以获得基于条约的安全保证。

data.en: The implementation of measures to achieve the objective of nuclear disarmament and non-proliferation of nuclear weapon shall not be interpreted as affecting the inalienable right of all States to develop, research, produce and use nuclear energy for peaceful purposes, without jeopardizing their respective fuel cycle policies, without discrimination and in conformity with articles I, II, III and IV of the Non-Proliferation Treaty, where applicable, and relevant safeguards agreements applied through the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) in order to prevent effectively the proliferation of nuclear weapons.
data.zh: 执行旨在实现核裁军和不扩散核武器目标的措施不应被解释为影响所有国家为和平目的，在不损害各自燃料循环政策、不受歧视且符合《不扩散条约》第一、第二、第三和第四条(如适用)以及通过国际原子能机构(原子能机构)为有效防止核武器扩散而适用的相关保障监督协定的情况下开发、研究、生产和使用核能的不可剥夺权利。

data.en: The principles and purposes contained in the United Nations Charter are fundamental to the pursuance of nuclear disarmament and non-proliferation of nuclear weapons.
data.zh: 《联合国宪章》所载的原则和宗旨对于实现核裁军和不扩散核武器至关重要。

data.en: Relevant legally binding instruments, coupled with other relevant unequivocal commitments and undertakings, relevant Security Council resolutions, the first resolution of the General Assembly, and the final document of the First Special Session of the General Assembly devoted to disarmament in 1978, provide a solid framework for multilateral efforts to achieve nuclear disarmament and prevent the proliferation of nuclear weapons.
data.zh: 具有法律约束力的相关文书，加上其他相关的明确承诺和保证、安全理事会相关决议、大会第一项决议以及1978年第一届专门讨论裁军问题的大会特别会议最后文件，为实现核裁军和防止核武器扩散的多边努力提供了坚实的框架。

data.en: Full and effective implementation of bilaterally and multilaterally negotiated legally-binding instruments on nuclear disarmament and non-proliferation of nuclear weapons by their States parties and signatories is essential in achieving a nuclear-weapon-free world.
data.zh: 缔约国和签署国充分有效地执行经双边和多边谈判达成的具有法律约束力的核裁军和不扩散核武器文书，对于实现无核武器世界至关重要。

data.en: Likewise, States undertake to pursue negotiations in good faith on effective measures relating to cessation of the nuclear arms race at an early date and to nuclear disarmament.
data.zh: 同样，各国承诺就早日停止核军备竞赛和核裁军的有效措施真诚地进行谈判。

data.en: III.
data.zh: 三.

data.en: Recommendations for achieving the objective of nuclear disarmament and non-proliferation of nuclear weapons
data.zh: 关于实现核裁军和不扩散核武器目标的建议

data.en: All States should remain committed to the shared objective of achieving and maintaining a world without nuclear weapons in a universal, verifiable, irreversible, transparent and non-discriminatory manner and pursue, in good faith, negotiations on nuclear disarmament and non-proliferation without further delay.
data.zh: 所有国家都应继续致力于以普遍、可核查、不可逆、透明和非歧视的方式实现和维持无核武器世界的共同目标，并真诚地就核裁军和不扩散进行谈判，不再拖延。

data.en: All nuclear-weapon States should do their utmost towards fulfilling their primary responsibility for nuclear disarmament, and other militarily significant States should also fulfil their responsibility for nuclear disarmament, while all non-nuclear-weapon States should contribute to this end.
data.zh: 所有核武器国家应尽最大努力履行其核裁军的主要责任，其他军事大国也应履行其核裁军的责任，而所有无核武器国家应为此作出贡献。

data.en: All States should urgently fulfil their respective unequivocal obligations and commitments on nuclear disarmament and non-proliferation, and pursue policies that are compatible with, and advance, these obligations and commitments.
data.zh: 所有国家都应紧急履行各自在核裁军和不扩散方面的明确义务和承诺，并奉行符合和促进这些义务和承诺的政策。

data.en: All States should adhere to international treaties to which they are party, and where appropriate, consider acceding to relevant international treaties on nuclear disarmament and non-proliferation.
data.zh: 所有国家都应遵守其加入的国际条约，并酌情考虑加入关于核裁军和不扩散的相关国际条约。

data.en: States are encouraged to adopt and implement, where applicable, regional treaties on nuclear weapons and their proliferation.
data.zh: 鼓励各国酌情通过和执行关于核武器及其扩散的区域条约。

data.en: States should also fully and reciprocally implement existing bilateral agreements, and further consider other bilateral agreements, in support of nuclear disarmament and non-proliferation.
data.zh: 各国还应全面和对等地执行现有双边协定，并进一步考虑其他双边协定，以支持核裁军和不扩散。

data.en: All States should sign and ratify the Comprehensive Nuclear-Test-Ban Treaty, particularly the remaining eight States listed in its Annex II that have yet to do so, recalling the special responsibility of nuclear-weapon States in this regard.
data.zh: 所有国家都应签署和批准《全面禁止核试验条约》，特别是附件二所列剩余八个尚未签署和批准该条约的国家，并回顾核武器国家在这方面的特殊责任。

data.en: Pending its entry-into-force, all States should commit to a moratorium on nuclear weapons test explosions or any other nuclear explosions, and refrain from any action contrary to the objective and aim of the treaty.
data.zh: 在该条约生效之前，所有国家都应承诺暂停核武器试爆或任何其他核爆炸，并避免采取任何违背该条约目标和宗旨的行动。

data.en: All States should adopt and enforce effective national laws to prohibit the proliferation of nuclear weapons and all other weapons of mass destruction, as well as their means of delivery, including to non-State actors, and to establish appropriate controls over related materials, in accordance with relevant United Nations resolutions.
data.zh: 所有国家都应通过和执行有效的国家法律，禁止核武器和所有其他大规模毁灭性武器及其运载工具的扩散，包括向非国家行为体的扩散，并根据联合国相关决议对有关材料建立适当的管制。

data.en: Non-proliferation measures should not jeopardize the full exercise of the inalienable rights of all States to apply and develop, their programme for the peaceful uses of nuclear energy for economic and social development in conformity with their priorities, interests and needs.
data.zh: 不扩散措施不应妨碍所有国家充分行使不可剥夺的权利，根据本国优先事项、利益和需求，应用和发展本国和平利用核能促进经济和社会发展的方案。

data.en: Disarmament and non-proliferation research and education is an invaluable tool to advance nuclear disarmament and non-proliferation in support of achieving and maintaining a world free of nuclear-weapon.
data.zh: 有关裁军和不扩散的研究和教育是推进核裁军和不扩散以支持实现和维持无核武器世界的宝贵工具。

data.en: States are encouraged to cooperate with the United Nations and other international organisations, as well as, where appropriate, relevant non-governmental organisations, academic institutions and other civil society groups, to take concrete measures to raise awareness of the public on all topics relating to nuclear disarmament and non-proliferation, and regarding the dangers of nuclear weapon and the imperatives to reach a world without nuclear weapons, including through interactions with and directly sharing experiences of the peoples and the communities affected by nuclear weapons use and testing to help understand the humanitarian and environmental impacts of nuclear weapons.
data.zh: 鼓励各国与联合国和其他国际组织合作，并酌情与相关非政府组织、学术机构和其他民间社会团体合作，采取具体措施提高公众对核裁军和不扩散所有相关议题的认识，以及对核武器危险和实现无核武器世界必要性的认识，包括为此与受核武器使用和试验影响的人民和社区互动并直接分享其经验，以帮助理解核武器的人道主义和环境影响。

data.en: States should engage, where appropriate, with efforts to address the rights and needs of affected communities through victim assistance, environmental remediation, and international cooperation and assistance.
data.zh: 各国应酌情通过受害者援助、环境补救以及国际合作和援助，努力满足受影响社区的权利和需求。

data.en: All States should strengthen nuclear disarmament and non-proliferation efforts by respecting and enhancing existing nuclear-weapon-free zones, including through signing and ratifying relevant protocols by concerned States, and establishing additional such zones, where appropriate, including a nuclear-weapon-free-zone in the Middle East, in accordance with the 1999 United Nations Disarmament Commission Principles and Guidelines.
data.zh: 所有国家都应加强核裁军和不扩散努力，尊重和加强现有无核武器区，包括由有关国家签署和批准相关议定书，并根据1999年联合国裁军审议委员会的原则和准则，酌情建立更多的无核武器区，包括中东无核武器区。

data.en: All States should do their utmost to implement immediately the resolution on the Middle East adopted at the 1995 Review and Extension Conference of the Parties to the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons.
data.zh: 所有国家都应尽最大努力立即执行1995年不扩散核武器条约缔约国审议和延期大会通过的关于中东的决议。

data.en: The establishment of a Middle East zone free of nuclear weapons and other weapons of mass destruction would greatly enhance peace and security in the Middle East, and in that regard, the States concerned should further implement General Assembly decision 73/546, entitled “Convening a conference on the establishment of a Middle East zone free of nuclear weapons and other weapons of mass destruction”, as an important step towards the establishment of such a zone, on the basis of arrangements freely arrived at among all the States of the region concerned.
data.zh: 建立中东无核武器和其他大规模毁灭性武器区将大大加强中东的和平与安全，在这方面，有关国家应进一步执行大会题为“召开建立中东无核武器和其他大规模毁灭性武器区会议”的第73/546号决定，作为在有关区域所有国家自由达成的安排基础上建立中东无核武器和其他大规模毁灭性武器区的一个重要步骤。

data.en: [Alt. para 22] States concerned should resume efforts towards the establishment of a Middle East zone free of nuclear weapons and other weapons of mass destruction, on the basis of arrangements freely arrived at among all the States of the region concerned, with the support and full engagement of the nuclear-weapon States, and taking into consideration the security concerns of all States in the region.
data.zh: [备选22] 有关国家应继续努力，在有关区域所有国家自由达成的安排基础上，在核武器国家的支持和充分参与下，并考虑到该区域所有国家的安全关切，建立中东无核武器和其他大规模毁灭性武器区。

data.en: Pending the total elimination of nuclear weapons, all States should uphold the principles that a nuclear war cannot be won and must never be fought, and that the use of or threat of use of nuclear weapon is inadmissible.
data.zh: 在彻底消除核武器之前，所有国家都应坚持核战争打不赢也永远打不得的原则，以及不允许使用或威胁使用核武器的原则。

data.en: Pending the total elimination of nuclear weapons, where such obligations do not yet exist, the nuclear-weapon States should take appropriate measures to assure all non-nuclear-weapon States against the use of or threat of use of nuclear weapons, including by reaffirming the importance of full adherence to national obligations and commitments relating to non-nuclear-weapon States either unilaterally or multilaterally against the use of or threat of use of nuclear weapons, and should pursue efforts to conclude effective arrangements to assure non-nuclear weapon States against the use of or threat of use of nuclear weapons.
data.zh: 在彻底消除核武器之前，如果这种义务尚不存在，核武器国家应采取适当措施，向所有无核武器国家保证不使用或威胁使用核武器，包括重申单方面或多边充分遵守与无核武器国家有关的国家义务和承诺的重要性，不使用或威胁使用核武器，并应努力缔结有效安排，向无核武器国家保证不使用或威胁使用核武器。

data.en: [Alt. para 24] Pending the total elimination of nuclear weapons, nuclear-weapon States should extend, reaffirm and strengthen negative security assurances provided to non-nuclear-weapon States and adhere fully to existing, and further consider additional, legally binding instruments, obligations and commitments relating to such assurances.
data.zh: [备选24] 在彻底消除核武器之前，核武器国家应扩大、重申和加强向无核武器国家提供的消极安全保证，充分遵守现有与此类保证有关的具有法律约束力的文书、义务和承诺，并进一步考虑其他此类文书、义务和承诺。

data.en: Given that a non-discriminatory, multilateral, and internationally and effectively verifiable treaty on fissile material in all its dimensions and aspects represents a contribution to the objective of nuclear disarmament and non-proliferation, all States call upon the Conference on Disarmament to start and conclude negotiations on such a treaty within the context of an agreed, comprehensive and balanced programme of work.
data.zh: 鉴于一项关于裂变材料所有层面和方面的非歧视、多边、可进行有效国际核查的条约是对核裁军和不扩散目标的贡献，所有国家促请裁军谈判会议在全面和平衡的商定工作方案范围内启动并完成关于这样一项条约的谈判。

data.en: All States should maintain appropriate and effective levels of nuclear safety and security in keeping with their primary responsibility for nuclear safety and security, and support the central role and the work of IAEA in international cooperation on the matter of nuclear safety and security.
data.zh: 所有国家都应根据其对核安全和核安保的主要责任，保持适当和有效的核安全和核安保水平，支持原子能机构在核安全和核安保国际合作中的核心作用和工作。

data.en: All States should emphasize the importance of the safety and security of peaceful nuclear facilities and material in all circumstances, including in armed conflict, and of the IAEA Director-General’s seven indispensable pillars on nuclear safety and security derived from the IAEA safety standards and nuclear security guidance.
data.zh: 所有国家都应强调和平核设施和核材料在所有情况下、包括在武装冲突中的安全和安保的重要性，以及原子能机构总干事根据原子能机构安全标准和核安保指南提出的核安全和核安保七大不可或缺支柱的重要性。

data.en: States should contribute to strengthening the role of IAEA, which is the sole competent international authority responsible for verifying compliance with its safeguards, while duly recognizing other authorities related to bilateral or regional safeguards, in accordance with comprehensive safeguards agreements and other relevant safeguards agreements, as well as the Statute of IAEA.
data.zh: 各国应促进加强原子能机构作为负责核查保障监督措施遵守情况的唯一主管国际机构的作用，同时根据全面保障监督协定和其他相关保障监督协定以及《原子能机构规约》，适当承认与双边或区域保障监督有关的其他权力。

data.en: All States should adhere to their respective IAEA safeguards agreements and, where applicable, the additional protocols thereto without further delay, and are encouraged to conclude safeguards agreements and, on a voluntary basis, additional protocols thereto, in order to verify the non-diversion of nuclear material from peaceful use to nuclear weapons.
data.zh: 所有国家都应毫不拖延地遵守各自的原子能机构保障监督协定并在适用情况下遵守相关附加议定书，鼓励各国缔结保障监督协定并在自愿基础上缔结相关附加议定书，以核查核材料未从和平用途转用于核武器。

data.en: States should identify and develop effective verification measures related to nuclear disarmament, including building on the ongoing work of the Group of Governmental Experts on Nuclear Disarmament Verification, as appropriate, in order to provide assurances of compliance with nuclear disarmament obligations and commitments to achieve and maintain a nuclear weapon-free-world.
data.zh: 各国应查明和制定与核裁军有关的有效核查措施，包括酌情借鉴核裁军核查问题政府专家组正在开展的工作，以便保证遵守核裁军义务和承诺，实现和维持无核武器的世界。

data.en: The nuclear-weapon States should fulfil their legal obligations and their unequivocal undertakings on nuclear disarmament and non-proliferation and take the following steps with the objective of complete elimination of nuclear weapons:
data.zh: 核武器国家应履行其在核裁军和不扩散方面的法律义务和明确承诺，并采取以下步骤，以期彻底消除核武器：

data.en: Not to transfer to any recipient whatsoever nuclear weapons or other nuclear explosive devices or control over such weapons or explosive devices, directly or indirectly; and not to assist, encourage or induce in any way any non-nuclear-weapon State to manufacture or otherwise acquire nuclear weapons or other nuclear explosive devices, or control over such weapons or explosive devices;
data.zh: 不直接或间接向任何接受方转让核武器或其他核爆炸装置或对此类武器或爆炸装置的控制权；不以任何方式协助、鼓励或诱导任何无核武器国家制造或以其他方式获取核武器或其他核爆炸装置，或对此类武器或爆炸装置的控制权；

data.en: To cease the qualitative improvement and development of nuclear-weapons systems, and cease the production of all types of nuclear weapons and their means of delivery, and production of fissionable material for nuclear weapons;
data.zh: 停止核武器系统的质量改进和发展，并停止生产所有类型的核武器及其运载工具以及用于核武器的裂变材料；

data.en: To undertake measures aimed at preventing the use of or threat of use of nuclear weapons;
data.zh: 采取措施防止使用或威胁使用核武器；

data.en: To reduce substantively all types of nuclear weapons, pending their total elimination;
data.zh: 在彻底消除核武器之前，实质性地减少所有类型的核武器；

data.en: To apply the principles of irreversibility, transparency and verifiability to all measures undertaken in the fulfilment of their nuclear disarmament obligations and commitments;
data.zh: 对履行核裁军义务和承诺方面采取的所有措施适用不可逆、透明和可核查的原则；

data.en: To conclude safeguards agreements with IAEA for enabling the Agency to verify the fulfilment of their nuclear disarmament obligations, including to prevent further diversion of nuclear material from peaceful uses to nuclear weapons or other nuclear explosive devices and to place under the Agency safeguards fissile materials transferred from military programmes;
data.zh: 与原子能机构缔结保障监督协定，使该机构能够核查核裁军义务的履行情况，包括防止核材料进一步从和平用途转用于核武器或其他核爆炸装置，并将从军事方案转来的裂变材料置于该机构的保障监督之下；

data.en: To bring into effect the security assurances provided by relevant protocols of the nuclear-weapon-free zone treaties and, in that regard, consider withdrawing any reservations or unilateral interpretative declarations that are incompatible with the object and purpose of such treaties.
data.zh: 落实无核武器区条约相关议定书提供的安全保证，并在这方面考虑撤回不符合这些条约目标和宗旨的任何保留或单方面解释性声明。

data.en: Pending the total elimination of nuclear weapons, nuclear-weapon States should further adopt and implement the following measures to reduce the risks associated with existing nuclear weapons:
data.zh: 在彻底消除核武器之前，核武器国家应进一步采取和执行以下措施，以减少与现有核武器相关联的风险：

data.en: Avoid irresponsible and dangerous rhetoric and actions that could increase tension and induce nuclear conflict;
data.zh: 避免可能加剧紧张局势和引发核冲突的不负责任和危险的言论和行动；

data.en: Diminish, and ultimately eliminate, the role and significance of nuclear weapons in military strategies, concepts, policies or doctrines;
data.zh: 降低并最终消除核武器在军事战略、概念、政策或理论中的作用和重要性；

data.en: Apply existing and, as appropriate, develop new bilateral and multilateral strategic risk-reduction mechanisms, including relevant arrangements on information exchange, communication channels, missile de-targeting and launch notifications, and incident and crisis prevention and resolution;
data.zh: 应用现有的并酌情开发新的双边和多边减少战略风险机制，包括关于信息交流、沟通渠道、导弹取消瞄准和发射通知、事件和危机预防和解决的相关安排；

data.en: To reduce immediately the operational status of nuclear weapons, including through complete de-targeting and de-alerting, in order to avoid the risks of unintentional and accidental use of such weapons;
data.zh: 立即降低核武器的作战状态，包括完全不再瞄准目标和解除待命状态，以避免无意和意外使用这种武器的风险；

data.en: Sustain formalized communication dialogue in both multilateral and bilateral forums and conduct regular exchange of views on strategic risk reduction among themselves and, where relevant, with non-nuclear-weapon States;
data.zh: 在多边和双边论坛上保持正式的沟通对话，就减少战略风险问题定期交换意见，并酌情与无核武器国家交换意见；

data.en: Improve transparency about their nuclear policies and doctrines, as well as accidents involving nuclear weapons, including through reporting mechanisms such as those established by the relevant treaties, the decisions made in their review processes, and the relevant resolutions and decisions adopted by the General Assembly and other relevant intergovernmental bodies.
data.zh: 提高核政策、核理论和涉及核武器的事故的透明度，包括为此利用报告机制，例如相关条约、审查过程中作出的决定以及大会和其他相关政府间机构通过的相关决议和决定所设立的报告机制。

data.en: The present paper is presented under the Chair’s own responsibility and without prejudice to the position of any delegations.
data.zh: 本文件由主席自行提交，不影响任何代表团的立场。

data.en: It reflects the substance and status of the deliberations by delegations participating in the 2018–2020 cycle of the Working Group, which was extended to 2023.
data.zh: 文件反映了参加工作组2018-2020年周期(已延长至2023年)的各代表团的审议内容和状况。

data.en: As there was no final consensus on the recommendations as a result of widely differing views, the text does not necessarily represent agreement on any of the specific content.
data.zh: 由于意见分歧很大，没有就建议达成最后共识。

data.en: However, on the basis of the valuable discussions and proposals, the text may serve as a useful point of reference for any further work that may be mandated to future working groups on this subject matter.
data.zh: 本文不一定表示就任何具体内容达成一致，但根据宝贵的讨论和提议，本文可作为今后可能授权工作组就这一主题事项开展任何进一步工作的有益参考。

data.zh: 2023年届会

data.en: Agenda item 12 (e)
data.zh: 议程项目12 (e)

data.en: Coordination, programme and other questions: long-term programme of support for Haiti
data.zh: 协调、方案和其他问题：长期支助海地方案

data.en: Letter dated 7 February 2023 from the Permanent Representative of Saint Kitts and Nevis to the United Nations addressed to the President of the Economic and Social Council
data.zh: 2023年2月7日圣基茨和尼维斯常驻联合国代表给经济及社会理事会主席的信

data.en: I have the honour to write to you in your capacity as President of the Economic and Social Council and in recognition of the fraternal and historical bonds that exist between Saint Kitts and Nevis and Haiti as a neighbour, friend and brother within the Caribbean Community.
data.zh: 谨致函经济及社会理事会主席阁下，并确认作为加勒比共同体内的邻国、朋友和兄弟的圣基茨和尼维斯与海地之间的兄弟情谊和历史纽带。

data.en: In this connection, I wish to inform you that the Government of Saint Kitts and Nevis is keenly interested in joining the Ad Hoc Advisory Group on Haiti which plays a central role in the global efforts seeking a lasting solution to the multiple crises faced by Haiti and to bolster national efforts to bring peace, prosperity and socioeconomic well-being to its people.
data.zh: 就此，谨告知圣基茨和尼维斯政府非常有兴趣加入海地问题特设咨询小组，该小组在寻求持久解决海地面临的多重危机以及支持国家努力为海地人民带来和平、繁荣和社会经济福祉的全球努力中发挥着核心作用。

data.en: Saint Kitts and Nevis intends to meaningfully contribute to and strengthen the efforts of the Advisory Group to address the humanitarian needs of the population, restore security and political stability and devise a people-centred development pathway to reconstruction and good governance.
data.zh: 圣基茨和尼维斯打算切实促进和加强咨询小组的努力，以满足人民的人道主义需求，恢复安全和政治稳定，并制定以人为本的发展道路，以实现重建和善治。

data.en: Saint Kitts and Nevis is particularly keen to work within the Advisory Group to effectively treat the root causes of seemingly perpetual conflict and instability and to develop a framework of cooperation, technical support, institution-building and political engagement that enlists and mobilizes all stakeholders across all sectors of Haitian society as the drivers of decisive change and comprehensive national transformation.
data.zh: 圣基茨和尼维斯特别希望在咨询小组内开展工作，以有效处理似乎是永久性冲突和不稳定的根源，并制定一个合作、技术支持、机构建设和政治参与的框架，争取和动员海地社会各部门的所有利益攸关方，使其成为决定性变革和全面国家转型的驱动力。

data.en: Through peer-leadership, the use of regional convening power and the leveraging of its influence to rally international support, Saint Kitts and Nevis hopes to lend its support to the actualization and advancement of the sacred principles of equality, fraternity and liberty that were at the centre of Haiti’s movement for freedom and independence.
data.zh: 通过同侪领导、利用区域召集力并利用其影响力来争取国际支持，圣基茨和尼维斯希望为实现和推进平等、博爱和自由的神圣原则提供支持，这些原则是海地争取自由和独立运动的核心。

data.en: Having received the full endorsement of the Permanent Representative of Haiti to the United Nations, I should be grateful if you could have the present letter circulated to the members of the Economic and Social Council.
data.zh: 本函已得到海地常驻联合国代表的充分认可，请将本函分发给经济及社会理事会成员为荷。

data.en: (Signed) Nerys Dockery
data.zh: 内里斯·多克里(签名)

data.zh: 常驻代表

data.zh: 社会和人权问题：社会发展

data.en: Social and human rights questions: social development
data.zh: 2023年6月7日经济及社会理事会决议

data.en: Resolution adopted by the Economic and Social Council on 7 June 2023
data.zh: [根据社会发展委员会的建议(E/2023/26)通过]

data.en: Fourth review and appraisal of the Madrid International Plan of Action on Ageing, 2002
data.zh: 经济及社会理事会，

data.en: The Economic and Social Council,
data.zh: 回顾2002年4月8日至12日在马德里举行的第二次老龄问题世界大会通过的2002年《马德里老龄问题国际行动计划》， 要求会员国对其执行情况进行系统审查，这对于成功改善老年人生活质量至关重要，

data.en: Recalling that, in the Madrid International Plan of Action on Ageing, 2002, adopted by the Second World Assembly on Ageing, held in Madrid from 8 to 12 April 2002, the systematic review of its implementation by Member States was requested as being essential for its success in improving the quality of life of older persons,
data.zh: 又回顾大会2022年12月15日第77/190号决议和以往关于老龄问题的各项决议，以及人权理事会2022年10月6日第51/4号决议 和以往关于老龄问题的各项决议，

data.en: Recalling also General Assembly resolution 77/190 of 15 December 2022 and previous Assembly resolutions on ageing, as well as Human Rights Council resolution 51/4 of 6 October 2022 and previous Human Rights Council resolutions on ageing,
data.zh: 还回顾，经济及社会理事会2003年7月21日第2003/14号决议邀请各国政府、联合国系统和民间社会共同参与，以“由下而上”的方式审查和评价《马德里行动计划》，

data.en: Recalling further that the Economic and Social Council, in its resolution 2003/14 of 21 July 2003, invited Governments, the United Nations system and civil society to participate in a bottom-up approach to the review and appraisal of the Madrid Plan of Action,
data.zh: 铭记社会发展委员会2004年2月13日第42/1号决议 决定每五年对《马德里行动计划》进行一次审查和评价，

data.en: Bearing in mind that the Commission for Social Development, in its resolution 42/1 of 13 February 2004, decided to undertake the review and appraisal of the Madrid Plan of Action every five years,
data.zh: 表示赞赏地注意到秘书长的报告， 报告是在《马德里行动计划》第四次审查和评价基础上编写而成，

data.en: Taking note with appreciation of the report of the Secretary-General, which was based on the results of the fourth review and appraisal of the Madrid Plan of Action,
data.zh: 承认老龄问题不限成员名额工作组的工作及其对《马德里行动计划》的执行和后续行动所作的贡献，

data.en: Acknowledging the work of the Open-ended Working Group on Ageing and its contribution to the implementation of and follow-up to the Madrid Plan of Action,
data.zh: 又承认人权理事会任命的老年人享有所有人权问题独立专家在评价执行《马德里行动计划》对人权的影响方面所做的工作，

data.en: Acknowledging also the work of the Independent Expert on the enjoyment of all human rights by older persons, appointed by the Human Rights Council, on the assessment of the human rights implications of the implementation of the Madrid Plan of Action,
data.zh: 回顾《2030年可持续发展议程》， 其中包括若干专门针对老年人的远大全球目标，强调指出必须确保在执行议程时考虑到与老年人相关的问题，以确保不让任何一个人掉队，包括不让老年人掉队，并认识到需要将老龄问题纳入发展规划、政策、社会保障计划和方案的主流，

data.en: Recalling the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development, which includes several aspirational and global targets that are specific to older persons, stressing the importance of ensuring that issues of relevance to older persons are taken into account in its implementation in order to ensure that no one is left behind, including older persons, and recognizing the need to mainstream ageing in development planning, policies, social protection schemes and programmes,
data.zh: 注意到2027年将是第二次老龄问题世界大会二十五周年，并承认世界大会对实现不分年龄人人共享的社会的重要性，

data.en: Noting that 2027 will mark the twenty-fifth anniversary of the Second World Assembly on Ageing, and acknowledging the importance of the World Assembly to the realization of a society for all ages,
data.zh: 回顾大会2020年12月14日第75/131号决议宣布联合国促进老龄健康十年(2021-2030)，并认识到《马德里老龄问题国际行动计划》、联合国促进老龄健康十年和《2030年可持续发展议程》之间的协同增效作用，

data.en: Recalling the proclamation of the United Nations Decade of Healthy Ageing (2021–2030) in General Assembly resolution 75/131 of 14 December 2020, and recognizing the synergies between the Madrid International Plan of Action on Ageing, the United Nations Decade of Healthy Ageing and the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development,
data.zh: 注意到，到2050年，全世界出生时预期寿命预计将达到77.2岁；预计全球60岁或60岁以上人口人数将是全世界5岁以下儿童人数的三倍以上，比青年人人数高出约三分之二；而且在发展中世界增幅最大，增速最快，认识到需要更加关注包括人权领域在内的影响老年人的具体挑战，

data.en: Noting that, by 2050, life expectancy at birth is projected to reach 77.2 years worldwide, that the number of persons aged 60 years or over is projected to be more than three times the number of children under age 5 and about two thirds higher than the number of youth worldwide, and that this increase will be the greatest and the most rapid in the developing world, and recognizing that greater attention needs to be paid to the specific challenges affecting older persons, including in the field of human rights,
data.zh: 认识到人口老龄化是可能影响实现《2030年可持续发展议程》和所有可持续发展目标前景的主要趋势之一，还认识到执行《马德里行动计划》的优先方向和行动建议以及相关的区域执行战略对于实现可持续发展目标比以往任何时候都更加重要，有助于努力不让任何一个人掉队，特别是老年人，

data.en: Recognizing population ageing as one of the major trends that are likely to shape the prospects for achieving the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development and all of the Sustainable Development Goals, and recognizing also that implementing the priority directions and recommendations for action of the Madrid Plan of Action and the relevant regional implementation strategies are now more vital than ever to achieve the Sustainable Development Goals and contribute to efforts to leave no one behind, particularly older persons,
data.zh: 认识到冠状病毒病疫情对老年人，特别是老年妇女和老年残疾人造成了非常严重影响，应对此疫情和其他保健紧急情况需要尊重老年人的尊严，促进他们的自理，促进和保护他们的人权，并考虑到一切形式的暴力、歧视、污名化、排斥和不平等以及虐待、忽视、社会孤立和孤独，

data.en: Recognizing that the coronavirus disease (COVID-19) pandemic has had a disproportionately heavy impact on older persons, in particular older women and older persons with disabilities, and that responses to the COVID-19 pandemic and other health emergencies need to respect their dignity and promote their autonomy, promote and protect their human rights and take into account all forms of violence, discrimination, stigmatization, exclusion, inequalities, as well as abuse, neglect, social isolation and loneliness,
data.zh: 强调指出，为补充国家发展努力，必须加强不同形式、不同方式的国际合作，支持发展中国家执行《马德里行动计划》，应赞赏每个社会在老龄问题上的良好做法，不论其发展状况如何，

data.en: Stressing that, in complementing national development efforts, enhanced international cooperation, in its different forms and ways, is essential to support developing countries in implementing the Madrid Plan of Action, and that good practices related to ageing in each society should be appreciated regardless of their development status,
data.zh: 认识到会员国以及老龄问题机构间工作组等联合国相关机制、各机构和组织、政府间组织和非政府组织以及国家人权机构为执行《马德里行动计划》所采取的步骤，

data.en: Recognizing the steps taken in the implementation of Madrid Plan of Action by Member States, as well as relevant United Nations mechanisms, such as the Inter‑Agency Group on Ageing, bodies and organizations, intergovernmental and non-governmental organizations and national human rights institutions,
data.zh: 又认识到会员国必须根据本国立法并遵循适用的国际法，采取具体措施，按照《马德里行动计划》和《2015-2030年仙台减少灾害风险框架》，进一步保护和协助处于紧急情况中的老年人， 并提高这方面的认识，

data.en: Recognizing also that it is important for Member States to take concrete measures, in accordance with national legislation and consistent with applicable international law, to further protect and assist older persons in emergency situations, consistent with the Madrid Plan of Action and the Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction 2015–2030, and to raise awareness in this area,
data.zh: 还认识到需要将性别视角纳入有关老年人的所有政策和方案，以便考虑到老年妇女和老年男子的具体需要和经历，

data.en: Recognizing further the need to incorporate a gender perspective in all policies and programmes concerning older persons in order to take into account the specific needs and experiences of older women and men,
data.zh: 认识到处理国家和区域各级培训、能力建设、政策拟订和监测工作的各个国际和区域组织在促进和协助执行《马德里行动计划》方面的重要作用，肯定世界各地和区域举措开展的工作，包括2022年6月1日和2日在贝鲁特举行的西亚经济社会委员会《马德里国际行动计划》第四次区域审查会议；2022年6月16日和17日在罗马举行的第五届欧洲经济委员会老龄问题部长级会议；2022年7月12日和13日在亚的斯亚贝巴举行的《马德里老龄问题国际行动计划》第四次非洲区域审查会议；2022年6月29日至7月1日在曼谷和在线上举行的《马德里老龄问题国际行动计划》第四次审查和评价亚太政府间会议；2022年12月13日至15日在圣地亚哥举行的第五届拉丁美洲和加勒比地区老龄和老年人权利问题区域政府间会议，以及马耳他国际老龄问题研究所和维也纳欧洲社会福利政策和研究中心等研究机构所开展的工作，

data.en: Also requests the Secretary-General to submit to the Commission for Social Development at its sixty-third session, in 2025, a report on the modalities for the fifth review and appraisal of Madrid International Plan of Action and the implementation of the present resolution.

data.zh: 摘要

data.en: Summary
data.zh: 世界经济正面临严重的阻力，增长前景疲弱，通胀率上升，不确定性增加。

data.en: The world economy is facing severe headwinds amid weak growth prospects, elevated inflation and heightened uncertainties.
data.zh: 尽管全球经济形势显示出轻微改善迹象，但多种因素的共同作用——冠状病毒病(COVID-19)大流行的遗留影响、旷日持久的乌克兰战争、气候变化的影响不断恶化以及宏观经济状况的快速变化——正在给经济前景蒙上阴影。

data.en: A confluence of factors – legacy effects of the coronavirus disease (COVID-19) pandemic, the lingering war in Ukraine, the ever worsening impact of climate change and rapidly shifting macroeconomic conditions – are clouding the economic outlook, even as the global picture shows slight signs of improvement.
data.zh: 持续高企的通胀引发了数十年来最激进的加息，导致金融条件急剧收紧，并加剧了债务脆弱性。

data.en: Persistently high inflation has prompted the most aggressive interest rate hikes in decades, causing financial conditions to tighten sharply and exacerbating debt vulnerabilities.
data.zh: 尽管经济前景仍然低迷，但2023年全球增长放缓的程度可能没有此前预期的那么严重，主要原因是发达经济体的家庭支出具有弹性，以及中国的复苏。

data.en: While economic prospects remain subdued, the slowdown in global growth in 2023 is likely to be less severe than previously expected, owing mainly to resilient household spending in developed economies and recovery in China.
data.zh: 目前预计2023年全球经济增长将从2022年的3.1%放缓至2.3%，较1月份的预测上调0.4个百分点。

data.en: Global growth is now projected to slow from 3.1 per cent in 2022 to 2.3 per cent in 2023, an upward revision by 0.4 percentage points from the January forecast.
data.zh: 预计2023年全球通胀将从2022年的7.5%降至5.2%，主要原因是食品和能源价格下降以及全球需求疲软。

data.en: Global inflation is projected to decline from 7.5 per cent in 2022 to 5.2 per cent in 2023, owing mainly to lower food and energy prices and softening global demand.
data.zh: 在通货膨胀压力缓解的情况下，预计全球经济将在2024年恢复一些势头，但预计增长率处于2.5%的水平，这仍将远低于3.1%的长期(2000-2019年)平均水平。

data.en: Amid easing inflationary pressures, the global economy is expected to pick up some momentum in 2024, but at 2.5 per cent, growth is projected to remain well below the longer-term (2000–2019) average of 3.1 per cent.
data.zh: 在出现多重相互关联的危机和宏观经济不确定性加剧的背景下，货币和财政政策挑战进一步加剧。

data.en: Against a backdrop of multiple interconnected crises and heightened macroeconomic uncertainties, monetary and fiscal policy challenges have further intensified.
data.zh: 美利坚合众国和欧洲最近银行部门的动荡暴露了金融系统的脆弱性，使中央银行在打击通货膨胀和维护金融稳定之间的权衡复杂化。

data.en: The recent banking sector turmoil in the United States of America and Europe has revealed fragilities in the financial system, complicating the trade-off for central banks between fighting inflation and preserving financial stability.
data.zh: 发达国家实行了十年的宽松货币政策，低利率和量化宽松，鼓励了金融部门过度杠杆化，产生了负面的全球溢出效应，但高利率和量化紧缩的前景现在对发展中国家构成了巨大挑战。

data.en: After a decade of loose monetary policy with low interest rates and quantitative easing in developed countries, which encouraged excessive leverage in the financial sector and generated negative global spillovers, the prospect of high interest rates and quantitative tightening now poses a massive challenge for developing countries.
data.zh: 由于无法获得负担得起的资金，许多政府投资于教育、卫生、可持续基础设施和能源转变的能力受到限制，同时有可能迫使越来越多的国家陷入债务违约。

data.en: Lack of access to affordable finance limits the ability of many Governments to invest in education, health, sustainable infrastructure and the energy transition, while threatening to push a growing number of countries into debt default.
data.zh: 一.

data.en: I.
data.zh: 全球宏观经济趋势

data.en: Global macroeconomic trends
data.zh: 全球概览

data.en: Uncertainties and poor growth prospects continue to bedevil the world economy.
data.zh: 尽管全球形势略有改善，但冠状病毒病(COVID-19)大流行的长期影响、旷日持久的乌克兰战争、气候变化日益恶化的影响以及日益严峻的政策挑战等因素的共同作用，将许多国家推向了崩溃的边缘。

data.en: A confluence of factors – the long reach of the coronavirus disease (COVID-19) pandemic, the persisting war in Ukraine, the ever worsening impacts of climate change and growing policy challenges – has pushed many countries to the brink, even as the global picture shows slight improvement.
data.zh: 在疫情之后，发达国家和发展中国家的通胀率居高不下，导致数十年来最激进的加息。

data.en: Stubbornly high inflation in both developed and developing countries in the aftermath of the pandemic prompted the most aggressive interest rate hikes in decades.
data.zh: 尽管利率上升，但家庭支出和就业——特别是在发达经济体——一直保持着弹性，这使得央行更难遏制通胀。

data.en: Against this backdrop, the deceleration in global growth for 2023, as projected earlier, is likely to be less severe than previously anticipated, owing mainly to persistently robust household spending in the largest economies, especially the United States of America and the European Union, as well as the recovery in China.
data.zh: 目前预计2023年全球增长将从2022年的3.1%放缓至2.3%(从1月份预测的1.9%上调)(图一)。

data.en: Global growth is now projected to slow from 3.1 per cent in 2022 to 2.3 per cent in 2023 (up from 1.9 per cent forecast in January) (figure I).
data.zh: 图一

data.en: Figure I
data.zh: 世界、发达经济体和发展中经济体经济产出增长率

data.en: Growth of economic output in the world, developed and developing economies
data.zh: (百分比)

data.en: (Percentage)

data.en: Source: United Nations, Department of Economic and Social Affairs, based on estimates produced with the World Economic Forecasting Model.
data.zh: 缩略语：e，估计；f，预测。

data.en: The global economy is predicted to pick up some momentum, expanding by 2.5 per cent in 2024, with inflationary pressures easing during the second half of 2023.
data.zh: 然而，这一增长率远低于3.1%的世界长期(2000-2019年)平均增长率。

data.en: That rate, however, is well below the world’s longer-term (2000–2019) average growth rate of 3.1 per cent.
data.zh: 由于结构性挑战，包括疫情带来的创伤、投资低迷、债务脆弱性加剧和资金短缺等问题仍未得到解决，世界经济面临长期增长低于平均水平的风险。

data.en: As structural challenges, including scarring from the pandemic, subdued investment, mounting debt vulnerabilities and funding shortages, remain unaddressed, the world economy is facing the risk of a prolonged period of subpar growth.
data.zh: 收入增长缓慢反过来又会进一步破坏在消除贫困和其他可持续发展目标方面取得进展的前景。

data.en: After declining steadily in 2022, consumer confidence improved slightly in recent months in most major economies, helped by lower international energy and food prices (figure IIa).
data.zh: 然而，消费者信心水平仍远低于其长期平均水平。

data.en: However, consumer confidence levels remain far below their long-term averages.
data.zh: 与此同时，以采购经理指数衡量的制造业活动似乎已经触底反弹(图二b)，采购经理指数在中国重新开放后有所上升。

data.en: At the same time, manufacturing activity, as measured by the Purchasing Managers Index, which saw an uptick following the reopening of China, appears to have bottomed out (figure IIb).
data.zh: 图二

data.en: Figure II

data.en: Note: The manufacturing Purchasing Managers Index (PMI) is a weighted average of five subindices: new orders, output, employment, suppliers’ delivery times and stocks of purchases.
data.zh: 国家组数据是单个国家PMI的加权平均值，由标普全球制作。

data.en: Country group data are weighted averages of individual country PMIs, produced by S&P Global.
data.zh: 该值低于50表明活动与前一个月相比有所收缩。

data.en: Meanwhile, global financial markets have remained largely resilient despite ongoing banking sector turmoil in the United States and Europe.
data.zh: 2023年3月，美国总资产排名第十六位的硅谷银行，以及标志银行(Signature Bank)倒闭，还有瑞士政府促成的对具有全球系统性重要性的银行瑞士信贷的收购，令全球金融市场动荡不安。

data.en: In March 2023, the collapse of Silicon Valley Bank, the sixteenth largest bank in the United States by total assets, and Signature Bank, as well as the Swiss government-brokered takeover of Credit Suisse, a globally systemically important bank, rattled financial markets worldwide.
data.zh: 5月初，美国政府控制了总资产为2 120亿美元的第一共和银行，并将其出售给摩根大通。

data.en: In early May, the United States Government seized control of First Republic Bank, with total assets of $212 billion, and sold it to JPMorgan Chase.
data.zh: 虽然各国政府和金融监管机构设法遏制了动荡，但这些事态发展表明，可能存在更多的系统性金融稳定风险。

data.en: While Governments and financial regulators managed to contain the turmoil, these developments show the potential of more systemic financial stability risks.
data.zh: 尽管市场动荡，美联储和其他发达国家央行继续提高政策利率，因为核心通胀率居高不下，且比预期更为持久。

data.en: Despite the market turmoil, the Federal Reserve and other developed country central banks continued to raise policy rates as core inflation has remained high and more persistent than expected.
data.zh: 尽管主要央行已放缓货币紧缩步伐，但2023年仍有可能进一步加息。

data.en: The slightly improved outlook for global growth in 2023 reflects primarily upward revisions in the major developed countries and China (see table below).
data.zh: 在美国，事实证明消费者支出和非居住用投资比预期更具弹性，促使2023年增长预测上调至1.1%(从1月份预测的0.4%上调)。

data.en: In the United States, consumer spending and non-residential investment have proved more resilient than expected, prompting upward revision of the growth forecast to 1.1 per cent in 2023 (up from 0.4 per cent forecast in January).
data.zh: 然而，随着金融条件收紧和房价进一步调整，消费者支出预计将放缓，拖累增长前景。

data.en: However, amid tightening financial conditions and further adjustments in house prices, consumer spending is projected to soften, weighing on growth prospects.
data.zh: 在欧洲，较低的天然气价格和强劲的消费者支出，特别是服务业支出，阻止了1月份预测的大幅放缓。

data.en: In Europe, lower gas prices and robust consumer spending, especially on services, have prevented the sharp slowdown that had been forecast in January.
data.zh: 欧洲联盟经济目前预计将在2023年增长0.9%(从1月份预测的0.2%上调)。

data.en: The economy of the European Union is now projected to grow by 0.9 per cent in 2023 (up from 0.2 per cent forecast in January).
data.zh: 在2022年12月解除与COVID-19大流行有关的限制后，中国国内生产总值(GDP)在2023年第一季度的扩张速度超过了预期。

data.en: After lifting restrictions related to the COVID-19 pandemic in December 2022, the gross domestic product (GDP) of China expanded faster than expected in the first quarter of 2023.
data.zh: 目前预测今年的年增长率为5.3%(从1月份预测的4.8%上调)。

data.en: Improved short-term prospects in the world’s three largest economies contrast with downward revisions to growth in many developing countries.
data.zh: 预计2023年非洲及拉丁美洲和加勒比的人均国内生产总值仅略有增长，从而强化了经济表现疲弱的长期趋势(图三)。

data.en: GDP per capita is projected to grow only marginally in Africa and Latin America and the Caribbean in 2023, reinforcing a long-term trend of weak economic performance (figure III).
data.zh: 不可持续的偿债负担、不断增加的国际收支压力、不断扩大的融资缺口和有限的财政空间将继续破坏许多发展中国家的近期增长前景。

data.en: Unsustainable debt servicing burdens, mounting balance-of-payments pressures, growing financing gaps and constrained fiscal space will continue to undermine near-term growth prospects of many developing countries.
data.zh: 图三

data.en: Figure III

data.zh: 通货膨胀和商品价格

data.en: While inflation has been easing in recent months, it is expected to remain above central bank targets in 2023.
data.zh: 预计2023年全球通胀将从2022年的7.5%降至5.2%，主要原因是食品和能源价格下降以及全球需求疲软。

data.en: Global inflation is projected to decline from 7.5 per cent in 2022 to 5.2 per cent in 2023, owing mainly to lower food and energy prices and softening global demand.
data.zh: 然而，通货膨胀率仍将远高于2000-2019年3.1%的平均水平(图四)。

data.en: Global food prices have been falling since mid-2022 owing to several factors, including the resumption of exports from Ukrainian ports under the Initiative on the Safe Transportation of Grain and Foodstuffs from Ukrainian Ports.
data.zh: 2023年3月，联合国粮农组织食品价格指数年同比下降20%，降至126.9，为2021年7月以来的最低值。

data.en: The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations Food Price Index declined by 20 per cent year-on-year in March 2023 to 126.9, the lowest value since July 2021.
data.zh: 尽管石油输出国组织加(欧佩克+) 4月份意外减产，加上欧洲联盟对俄罗斯原油的禁令，对价格造成了暂时的上行压力，但油价继续下跌。

data.en: Although the unexpected production cut of the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries Plus (OPEC+) in April coupled with the European Union ban on Russian crude exerted temporary upward pressures on prices, oil prices continued to drop.
data.zh: 2023年1月至5月初，布伦特原油价格下跌16%，降至每桶75美元左右，为2021年12月以来的最低水平。

data.en: In developed countries, while headline inflation is expected to decline gradually from 7.8 per cent in 2022 to 4.8 per cent in 2023, it will remain well above central bank targets, typically about 2 per cent.
data.zh: 在美国，过去一年总通货膨胀率一直在放缓，2023年3月降至5.0%，为2021年5月以来的最低水平。

data.en: In the United States, headline inflation has been easing over the past year, falling to 5.0 per cent in March 2023, the lowest rate since May 2021.
data.zh: 在欧洲联盟，通货膨胀率在3月份降至8.3%，从卢森堡和西班牙的约3%到匈牙利的25.6%不等。

data.en: In the European Union, inflation declined to 8.3 per cent in March, ranging from about 3 per cent in Luxembourg and Spain to 25.6 per cent in Hungary.
data.zh: 虽然总通货膨胀率一直在下降，但美国和欧洲的核心通货膨胀率仍然很高，这主要是由服务价格上涨(例如住房、保险和运输)和工资强劲增长所推动的。

data.en: While headline inflation rates have been falling, core inflation in the United States and Europe remains high, driven mainly by rising service prices (for example, in housing, insurance and transport) and robust wage growth.
data.zh: 图四

data.en: Figure IV

data.en: Inflation is also trending downward in most developing countries amid lower commodity prices and reduced global supply constraints and depreciation pressures.
data.zh: 然而，年通货膨胀率仍将远高于长期平均数，特别是在西亚、南亚和非洲(图四)。

data.en: Annual inflation will, however, remain well above the long-term average, especially in Western Asia, South Asia and Africa (figure IV).
data.zh: 尽管全球粮食价格自2022年年中以来一直在下降，但由于进口成本居高不下、当地供应中断和市场不完善等多种因素，国内食品通胀往往居高不下。

data.en: Although global food prices have been declining since mid-2022, domestic food inflation has often stayed elevated owing to a number of factors, including still-high import costs, local supply disruptions and market imperfections.
data.zh: 根据世界银行的数据，2023年初，约90%的发展中国家的食品价格上涨仍保持在5%以上。

data.en: According to the World Bank, food inflation in early 2023 remained above 5 per cent in about 90 per cent of developing countries.
data.zh: 在拥有大量贫困人口的发展中国家，持续的高通货膨胀是消除贫困的又一个障碍。

data.en: Continuing high inflation in developing countries which are home to large numbers of people in poverty represents an additional barrier to poverty eradication.
data.zh: 来自受当前高粮食价格影响的国家的新证据再次证实了先前的证据，即妇女和儿童受由此造成的饥饿和营养不良的影响最严重。

data.en: Emerging evidence from countries affected by the current episode of high food prices reconfirms earlier evidence that women and children are the worst affected by the resulting hunger and malnutrition.
data.zh: 劳动力市场

data.en: Labour markets in Europe, Japan and North America have remained tight, with low unemployment rates and recurrent worker shortages.
data.zh: 疫情后，这些国家的劳动力供需不匹配，对工资造成上行压力，对努力降低通胀的中央银行构成额外的政策挑战。

data.en: Post-pandemic mismatches between labour supply and labour demand in these countries, exerting upward pressures on wages, pose additional policy challenges for central banks striving to bring down inflation.
data.zh: 2022年底，除美国及大不列颠及北爱尔兰联合王国外，发达经济体的就业率远高于疫情前的水平。

data.en: As shown in figure V, in many developed economies, employment rates increased more among women than among men, narrowing the gender gap in employment rates.
data.zh: 这是由于2019年至2020年期间，男性的就业率下降较快，2020年至2022年期间，男性的就业率恢复较弱，这可能是由多种因素造成的，包括就业部门的性别差异，以及远程工作和灵活工作安排等政策应对措施。

data.en: This is the result of a steeper decrease in the employment rate between 2019 and 2020 and a weaker recovery between 2020 and 2022 for men, which could be due to a combination of factors, including gender differences in the sector of employment, as well as policy responses, including telework and flexible working arrangements.
data.zh: 然而，尽管就业率方面的性别差距已经缩小，但妇女仍然承担着不成比例的无酬工作，这种差距在疫情期间一直存在。

data.en: In the United States, the unemployment rate declined to 3.5 per cent in March 2023, despite a steady increase in labour-force participation, which almost reached pre-pandemic levels.
data.zh: 然而，美联储的货币紧缩政策开始对建筑业、制造业和零售业等严重依赖银行信贷为其运营提供资金的行业的就业造成影响。

data.en: Monetary tightening by the Federal Reserve, however, started to take a toll on employment in sectors – construction, manufacturing and retail – that heavily rely on bank credit to finance their operations.
data.zh: 随着经济活动放缓，预计美国失业率在预测期内将略有上升，但按历史标准衡量仍将保持在较低水平。

data.en: As economic activity softens, the unemployment rate in the United States is projected to increase moderately during the forecast period but will remain low by historical standards.
data.zh: 在欧洲，许多国家的失业率也降至历史最低水平，2023年3月欧元区平均失业率为6.5%。

data.en: In Europe, unemployment has also fallen to record low levels in many countries, averaging 6.5 per cent in the euro area in March 2023.
data.zh: 尽管一些欧洲经济体可能会在2023年经历温和衰退，但劳动力市场状况预计将保持弹性，因为企业可能会在劳动力严重短缺的情况下保留工人。

data.en: Although some European economies may experience a mild recession in 2023, labour-market conditions are expected to remain resilient, as businesses will likely retain workers amid significant labour shortages.
data.zh: 图五

data.en: Figure V
data.zh: 2019年第四季度与2022年第四季度相比选定经济体就业率变化

data.en: Change in employment rates in selected economies, Q4 2019 vs Q4 2022
data.zh: (百分点)

data.en: (Percentage points)
data.zh: 资料来源：联合国经济和社会事务部，根据经济合作与发展组织(经合组织)的数据。

data.en: Source: United Nations, Department of Economic and Social Affairs, based on data from the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD).
data.zh: 注：该图显示了2019年第四季度至2022年第四季度15-64岁年龄组的就业率变化。

data.en: In China, the easing of pandemic-related restrictions has led to a marked decline in the urban unemployment rate, to about 5.5 per cent in early 2023.
data.zh: 不过，这仍高于大流行前4-5%的水平。

data.en: However, this is still above the pre-pandemic level of 4–5 per cent.
data.zh: 非洲许多地区的劳动力市场条件仍然具有挑战性，非正规就业率高，性别差距大，青年失业率上升。

data.en: Labour-market conditions remain challenging in many parts of Africa, with high rates of informality, gender gaps and rising youth unemployment.
data.zh: 从积极的方面来看，2022年南非失业率连续四个季度下降，达到两年来的最低点。

data.en: On the positive side, the unemployment rate in South Africa declined for four consecutive quarters in 2022, reaching a two-year low.
data.zh: 国际贸易

data.en: Global trade is expected to remain under pressure in the forecast period.
data.zh: 基线情景预测，2023年全球货物和服务贸易量将增长2.3%，略高于此前预测的近零增长。

data.en: The baseline scenario projects that the volume of global trade in goods and services will grow by 2.3 per cent in 2023, slightly higher than the previous forecast of near zero growth.
data.zh: 这反映了对国内生产总值增长预测的向上修正。

data.en: This reflects the upward revision to GDP growth projections.
data.zh: 此外，中国的重新开放预计将增加国内需求，并可能通过增加货物与服务进口来推动全球贸易。

data.en: Furthermore, the reopening of China is expected to increase domestic demand and potentially boost global trade with increased imports of goods and services.
data.zh: 然而，COVID-19大流行挥之不去的影响、地缘政治紧张局势加剧和货币紧缩将继续阻碍全球贸易，尽管供应链限制和高航运成本已经缓解(图六)。

data.en: However, the lingering effects of the COVID-19 pandemic, rising geopolitical tensions and monetary tightening will continue to hold back global trade, although supply chain constraints and high shipping costs have eased (figure VI).
data.zh: 在旅行和旅游业进一步复苏的支持下，服务贸易增长快于货物贸易。

data.en: Trade in services experienced faster growth than trade in goods, supported by further recovery in the travel and tourism sectors.
data.zh: 随着目的地和市场开放，2023年国际旅游业将巩固复苏，这得益于被压抑的需求，特别是来自亚太地区的需求。

data.en: International tourism is set to consolidate its recovery in 2023, backed by pent-up demand, particularly from Asia and the Pacific, as destinations and markets open up.
data.zh: 世界旅游组织估计，到2023年，国际游客人数可能达到疫情前水平的80%至95%。

data.en: The World Tourism Organization estimates that international tourism arrivals could reach 80–95 per cent of pre-pandemic levels in 2023.
data.zh: 图六

data.en: Figure VI
data.zh: 全球供应链压力指数和全球集装箱运价指数

data.en: Global supply chain pressure index and global container freight index
data.zh: 资料来源：联合国经济和社会事务部，根据纽约联邦储备银行和环亚经济数据库的数据。

data.en: Source: United Nations, Department of Economic and Social Affairs, based on data from the Federal Reserve Bank of New York and CEIC.
data.zh: 缩略语：LHS，左标尺；RHS，右标尺。

data.en: Abbreviations: LHS, left-hand scale; RHS, right-hand scale.
data.zh: 国际金融市场

data.en: With energy prices and inflation gradually softening, international capital markets have been expecting a pause or even a reversal of monetary tightening in the developed economies.
data.zh: 尽管这一点仍然难以捉摸，但通胀放缓的前景降低了投资者的规避风险情绪，也降低了长期利率。

data.en: While this has remained elusive, prospects of slowing inflation have reduced investors’ risk aversion as well as long-term interest rates.
data.zh: 尽管波动很大，但流向发展中国家的资本有所恢复(图七)，扭转了2022年上半年的下降趋势。

data.en: Capital flows to developing countries recovered (figure VII), albeit with significant volatility, reversing the decline in the first half of 2022.
data.zh: 2023年1月，新兴经济体新发行的主权债券总额为398亿美元，为有记录以来的第二高水平。

data.en: New sovereign bond issuances from emerging economies totalled $39.8 billion in January 2023, the second-highest level on record.
data.zh: 许多发展中国家的货币也收复了2022年大部分时间遭受的部分损失。

data.en: Many developing country currencies also recouped some of the losses suffered throughout most of 2022.
data.zh: 2022年10月至2023年3月，美元对新兴市场货币的名义指数下降了6%，但仍比2021年1月的水平高出约5%。

data.en: Between October 2022 and March 2023, the nominal United States dollar index against emerging market currencies fell by 6 per cent but it still remains about 5 per cent above the level of January 2021.
data.zh: 然而，随着主要央行近几个月进一步加息，全球金融条件持续收紧。

data.en: However, as major central banks further increased interest rates in recent months, global financial conditions continued to tighten.
data.zh: 图七

data.en: Figure VII
data.zh: 流向新兴经济体的非居民投资组合

data.en: Non-resident portfolio flows to emerging economies
data.zh: (单位：10亿美元)

data.en: (Billions of United States dollars)
data.zh: 资料来源：联合国经济和社会事务部，根据国际金融协会的数据。

data.en: Source: United Nations, Department of Economic and Social Affairs, based on data from the Institute of International Finance (IIF).
data.zh: 注：关于被列为新兴经济体的国家的重点可在国际金融协会网站上查阅。

data.en: In March 2023, the collapse of Silicon Valley Bank and Signature Bank in the United States and the near failure of Credit Suisse sent shock waves through the global financial sector.
data.zh: 利率的快速上升加剧了资产负债的不匹配，暴露了这些破产银行资产负债表的脆弱性和风险管理的失败。

data.en: Rapidly rising interest rates exacerbated asset-liability mismatches and exposed balance sheet vulnerabilities and failures in risk management in the failed banks.
data.zh: 对危机蔓延的恐惧促使监管机构迅速采取果断行动，将对金融稳定的威胁降到最低。

data.en: The fear of contagion prompted swift and decisive actions by regulators, which minimized any threats to financial stability.
data.zh: 在美国，联邦存款保险公司将存款保护范围扩大到这些破产银行的所有储户，而不仅仅是存款低于25万美元保险限额的储户。

data.en: In the United States, the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation expanded deposit protection to all depositors at the failed banks instead of only those whose deposits were below the $250,000 insurance limit.
data.zh: 美联储短暂逆转了2022年4月开始的量化紧缩计划下的资产负债表萎缩。

data.en: The Federal Reserve briefly reversed its balance sheet shrinking under the quantitative tightening programme, which had been started in April 2022.
data.zh: 美联储在2022年4月11日至2023年3月6日期间将其资产负债表减少了6 230亿美元，它在硅谷银行倒闭之后，在2023年3月10日至27日期间将资产负债表扩大了3 640亿美元，以提供流动性并稳定金融业。

data.en: After having reduced its balance sheet by $623 billion between 11 April 2022 and 6 March 2023, the Federal Reserve expanded the balance sheet by $364 billion during the period 10–27 March 2023, following the failure of Silicon Valley Bank, to provide liquidity and stabilize the financial sector.
data.zh: 然而，银行业，尤其是美国银行业，仍然面临压力，2023年5月初第一共和银行倒闭就是明证。

data.en: However, the banking sector, especially in the United States, has remained under pressure, as evidenced by the failure of First Republic Bank in early May 2023.
data.zh: 地区性银行的动荡不会带来任何系统性风险，因为该国那些最大银行的资产负债表仍然强劲，但短期内可能会进一步限制信贷增长。

data.en: Financial conditions in emerging economies with good credit ratings have generally remained relatively stable in the aftermath of such turbulence.
data.zh: 然而，一些经济体的信贷息差上升，进一步限制了它们获得融资的机会。

data.en: However, some economies experienced a rise in credit spreads, further limiting their access to finance.
data.zh: 3月份，“前沿市场”的信贷息差扩大了120个基点。

data.en: In March, credit spreads in “frontier markets” widened by 120 basis points.
data.zh: 肯尼亚和尼日利亚等几个发展中经济体决定推迟发行新主权债券的计划，因为信贷息差大幅增加。

data.en: Several developing economies, for example, Kenya and Nigeria, decided to postpone plans to issue new sovereign bonds amid a significant increase in credit spreads.
data.zh: 根据国际货币基金组织(基金组织)的数据，目前有12种主权债券的不良交易利差超过1 000个基点，另有20种债券的交易利差超过700个基点。

data.en: According to the International Monetary Fund (IMF), there are currently 12 sovereign bonds trading at distressed spreads of more than 1,000 basis points and an additional 20 bonds trading at spreads of more than 700 basis points.
data.zh: 因此，发达经济体银行部门动荡的影响进一步损害了许多发展中经济体的融资条件。

data.en: The reverberations from the banking sector turmoil in the developed economies have thus further impaired financing conditions in many developing economies.
data.zh: 货币和财政政策：趋势和挑战

data.en: Central banks in developed and developing countries have continued to tighten monetary policy in 2023 to anchor inflation expectations and maintain credibility.
data.zh: 然而，随着总体通货膨胀数字逐渐缓和，大多数中央银行都放慢了加息步伐(图八)。

data.en: However, most central banks have reduced the pace of their interest rate hikes as headline inflation figures have gradually eased (figure VIII).
data.zh: 在美国，美联储在2022年前几次加息75个基点之后，今年1月、3月和5月仅加息25个基点，并暗示在收紧周期中会暂停。

data.en: In the United States, the Federal Reserve raised interest rates by only 25 basis points in January, March and May, after several earlier rate hikes of 75 basis points in 2022, and hinted at a pause in the tightening cycle.
data.zh: 欧洲央行在连续三次加息50个基点之后，在5月份也转向了较小幅度的加息(25个基点)。

data.en: The European Central Bank also shifted to a smaller hike (25 basis points) in May, following three consecutive hikes of 50 basis points.
data.zh: 几个发展中国家的中央银行也采取了更为谨慎的做法，而其他国家，特别是拉丁美洲国家的中央银行则暂停了加息。

data.en: Several developing country central banks have also adopted a more cautious approach, while others, especially in Latin America, have paused rate hikes.
data.zh: 图八

data.en: Figure VIII
data.zh: 按中央银行分列的政策利率变化

data.en: Policy rate changes by central banks
data.zh: (百分点)

data.en: (Percentage points)
data.zh: 资料来源：联合国经济和社会事务部，取自环亚经济数据库数据。

data.en: Source: United Nations, Department of Economic and Social Affairs, based on data from CEIC.
data.zh: 注：政策利率变动是指各时期和国家类别中所有中央银行政策变动的净总和。

data.en: Note: The policy rate changes refer to the net sum of all central banks’ policy changes in each period and country category.
data.zh: 这一数字涵盖了78家中央银行。

data.en: The recent banking sector turmoil in the United States and Europe has exposed the trade-offs between raising policy rates and preserving financial stability.
data.zh: 十年来的超宽松货币政策和接近零的政策利率鼓励了金融部门的过度杠杆化。

data.en: A decade of ultra-loose monetary policy and near zero policy rates has encouraged excessive leverage in the financial sector.
data.zh: 突然转向货币紧缩和加息，暴露了资产-负债错配，使金融业面临重大久期风险。

data.en: Over the forecast period, monetary policy stances are expected to diverge.
data.zh: 在主要发达经济体中，紧缩周期已经提前：美联储预计将在2023年再加息一次或两次，而在欧元区，利率可能在今年第三季度见顶。

data.en: In the major developed economies, the tightening cycle is well advanced: the Federal Reserve is projected to implement one or two more rate hikes in 2023, while in the euro area, interest rates may peak in the third quarter of the year.
data.zh: 发达经济体结束货币紧缩将使一些发展中国家的中央银行能够重新调整其货币立场，以支持经济增长，而其他国家的中央银行将在通胀居高不下的情况下继续加息。

data.en: In 2022, fiscal trends across the world were driven by the continuing economic recovery from the COVID-19 crisis, the impact of unexpected inflation on debt dynamics (which benefited developed economies in particular) and tighter fiscal stances, as pandemic-related support measures were phased out.
data.zh: 发达国家和发展中国家的平均财政赤字和公共债务数量占国内生产总值的比例连续第二年下降。

data.en: Average fiscal deficits and public debt levels as a share of GDP declined for a second consecutive year in both developed and developing countries.
data.zh: 商品生产国，特别是石油资源丰富的国家，在财政业绩方面有了特别大的改善。

data.en: Commodity producers, especially oil-rich countries, experienced particularly large improvements in fiscal performance.
data.zh: 2022年全球公共债务占国内生产总值的比例估计为92.1%，比2020年低7.6个百分点，但仍明显高于疫情前的84.3%。

data.en: These aggregate short-term trends should not obscure an increasingly challenging fiscal outlook, especially for developing countries facing weak and diminished growth prospects.
data.zh: 自2022年初以来，全球货币政策大幅收紧，显著加剧了许多国家的财政和债务脆弱性，并进一步限制了财政空间，特别是在撒哈拉以南非洲、南亚以及拉丁美洲和加勒比地区。

data.en: The aggressive tightening of global monetary policy since early 2022 has significantly exacerbated fiscal and debt vulnerabilities and further constrained fiscal space in many countries, especially in sub-Saharan Africa, South Asia, and Latin America and the Caribbean.
data.zh: 借款费用急剧上升，强势美元尽管近几个月有所走软，但仍推高了以美元计价的债务的偿债负担。

data.en: Borrowing costs have risen sharply and the strong dollar, despite some softening in recent months, has pushed up the debt-servicing burden of dollar-denominated debt.
data.zh: 在非洲，外债还本付息占政府收入的比例急剧上升，而获得发展援助和私人资金的机会减少。

data.en: In Africa, external debt service as a share of government revenue has risen sharply, while access to development assistance and private finance has diminished.
data.zh: 筹资方面的制约因素将限制政府投资于教育、卫生、可持续基础设施和能源转型以及加快实现可持续发展的能力，同时有可能迫使越来越多的国家陷入债务违约。

data.en: Financing constraints will limit the ability of Governments to invest in education, health, sustainable infrastructure and energy transition and accelerate progress towards sustainable development, while threatening to push a growing number of countries into debt default.
data.zh: 二.

data.en: II.
data.zh: 非常规货币政策的溢出效应

data.en: After decades of quiescence, high inflation made a comeback in 2022.
data.zh: 主要发达国家的中央银行开始提高利率，并通过出售其资产负债表上的资产来减少金融市场的流动性，展开一个被称为量化紧缩的进程。

data.en: Major developed country central banks began to raise interest rates and reduce liquidity in the financial market by selling off assets on their balance sheets, pursuing a process known as quantitative tightening.
data.zh: 然而，事实证明，鉴于多年来通过自2008年全球金融危机以来注入数万亿美元流动性的方案实施的超宽松的货币量化宽松政策，量化紧缩具有挑战性。

data.en: However, quantitative tightening has proved to be challenging, given the years of the ultra-loose monetary policy of quantitative easing, undertaken through programmes that have injected trillions of dollars of liquidity since the global financial crisis in 2008.
data.zh: 发达经济体从量化宽松向量化紧缩的过渡对许多发展中国家来说可能特别具有挑战性，因为这将大大收紧全球金融条件，增加其借款费用，并加剧债务可持续性风险。

data.en: Developed country central banks first resorted to unconventional monetary policy measures when policy rates reached or approached the zero lower bound.
data.zh: 量化宽松的目的是通过购买政府债券或其他金融资产来增加流动性和刺激经济活动。

data.en: The aim of quantitative easing was to increase liquidity and stimulate economic activity through the purchase of government bonds or other financial assets.
data.zh: 值得注意的是，日本银行为应对长期的经济停滞和通货紧缩，于2001年首次实施了量化宽松政策。

data.en: During the global financial crisis, quantitative easing, through asset purchases by central banks, became the monetary policy tool of choice.
data.zh: 在美国，美联储需要恢复“大到不能倒”的银行的资产负债表，因为其中许多银行持有数万亿美元的抵押担保证券，这些证券在危机前已经贬值。

data.en: In the United States, the Federal Reserve needed to restore the balance sheets of the banks that were “too big to fail”, as many of them held trillions of dollars of mortgage-backed securities which had lost value before the crisis.
data.zh: 美联储认识到，尽管降低政策利率是必要的，但单靠降低政策利率并不能克服持有这些证券的银行的资产负债表挑战。

data.en: The Federal Reserve recognized that, though necessary, lowering policy interest rates alone would not overcome the balance sheet challenges of the banks that held those securities.
data.zh: 市场流动性有限，无法支持抵押担保证券的价格。

data.en: There was limited liquidity in the market to support the prices of the mortgage-backed securities.
data.zh: 随着抵押担保证券的价格大幅下跌，长期利率飙升。

data.en: With prices of mortgage-backed securities falling sharply, long-term interest rates surged.
data.zh: 美联储开始购买那些抵押担保证券——没有市场参与者愿意购买和持有的证券——以支撑银行的资产负债表并降低长期利率。

data.en: The Federal Reserve began to buy those mortgage-backed securities – securities that no market participant wanted to buy and hold – to shore up the balance sheets of the banks and reduce long-term interest rates.
data.zh: 其他发达国家的中央银行，特别是欧洲中央银行和英格兰银行，在2008年危机期间效仿美联储的做法，支持其金融部门。

data.en: In the aftermath of the global financial crisis, quantitative easing has remained a key monetary policy tool for developed country central banks, with some changes in strategy, including the tapering of asset purchases, announced in 2013, and balance sheet normalization, announced in 2017, by the Federal Reserve.
data.zh: 在疫情期间，由于政策利率处于历史低位，许多央行实施了大规模量化宽松政策，作为刺激经济的手段，尽管商业银行的资产负债表仍然强劲。

data.en: During the pandemic, with record low policy rates, many central banks implemented massive quantitative easing as a means of providing economic stimulus, even though the balance sheets of commercial banks remained strong.
data.zh: 2020年1月至2021年12月，美联储、欧洲央行和英格兰银行的资产负债表上的资产规模增加了一倍或接近一倍。

data.en: Between January 2020 and December 2021, the Federal Reserve, the European Central Bank and the Bank of England doubled or nearly doubled the assets on their balance sheets.
data.zh: 截至2021年底，美联储、欧洲中央银行、英格兰银行和日本银行的总资产分别达到约8.8万亿美元、9.6万亿美元、1.5万亿美元和6.3万亿美元(图九)，合计约占世界国内生产总值的四分之一。

data.en: As of the end of 2021, the total assets of the Federal Reserve, the European Central Bank, the Bank of England and the Bank of Japan reached about $8.8 trillion, $9.6 trillion, $1.5 trillion and $6.3 trillion, respectively (figure IX), collectively representing about one quarter of world GDP.
data.zh: 面对疫情期间全球金融条件恶化、资本大量外流及借款费用上升，27个发展中国家于2020年疫情期间首次推出量化宽松政策。

data.en: Facing deteriorating global financial conditions, large capital outflows and rising borrowing costs during the pandemic, 27 developing countries introduced quantitative easing for the first time during the pandemic in 2020.
data.zh: 图九

data.en: Figure IX
data.zh: 主要发达国家中央银行资产总额

data.en: Total assets of major developed country central banks
data.zh: (万亿美元)

data.en: (Trillions of United States dollars)
data.zh: 资料来源：联合国经济和社会事务部，根据美联储、欧洲中央银行、英格兰银行、日本银行、环亚经济数据库和《贸易经济学》的数据。

data.en: Source: United Nations, Department of Economic and Social Affairs, based on data from the Federal Reserve, the European Central Bank, the Bank of England, the Bank of Japan, CEIC and Trading Economics.
data.zh: 注：所有资产均按相应期间的汇率折算成美元。

data.en: Note: All assets are converted into United States dollars by using the exchange rate of the corresponding period.
data.zh: 发达国家：量化宽松的传导渠道和影响

data.en: With quantitative easing, the central banks pursued two objectives in the aftermath of the global financial crisis: (a) to restore financial stability and (b) to boost investment and economic growth by lowering long-term borrowing costs.
data.zh: 量化宽松通过多个渠道与实体经济互动：(a) 流动资金渠道，即增加货币供应和流动资金，以期减轻金融市场的压力，防止流动资金短缺，特别是在紧急危机阶段期间；(b) 投资组合再平衡渠道，即改变金融资产的相对价格，通过降低收益率鼓励投资者持有长期资产；及(c) 发出信号的渠道，即传达中央银行对稳定金融市场及支持经济的承诺。

data.en: Quantitative easing has been proved to be an important monetary policy tool for addressing financial distress, particularly during times of crisis.
data.zh: 2007年至2021年，美国广义货币占国内生产总值的比例从79.5%增至117%，欧元区从90%增至124%。

data.en: Between 2007 and 2021, broad money as a proportion of GDP increased from 79.5 to 117 per cent in the United States and from 90 to 124 per cent in the euro area.
data.zh: 多项研究发现，量化宽松可通过放宽银行融资限制、提高风险偏好和降低流动性溢价等方式改善市场流动性。 、

data.en: Quantitative easing had a large impact on bond yields.
data.zh: Krishnamurthy和Vissing-Jorgensen的研究表明，美联储的第一次和第三次量化宽松降低了抵押担保证券和国债收益率。

data.en: Research by Krishnamurthy and Vissing-Jorgensen illustrates that the first and third quantitative easings by the Federal Reserve decreased mortgage-backed securities and Treasury yields.
data.zh: 此外，在美国，在量化宽松之前拥有更多抵押担保证券的银行，其对私营部门的贷款增长速度快于那些少量拥有或根本没有抵押担保证券的银行，这表明量化宽松能够刺激额外的信贷供应。

data.en: Moreover, in the United States, banks that owned more mortgage-backed securities prior to quantitative easing had faster growth in loans to the private sector than those that had little or no holdings of mortgage-backed securities, indicating that quantitative easing was able to stimulate additional credit provision.
data.zh: 然而，在许多其他因素发挥作用的情况下，较低的收益率和额外的信贷不一定能支持中长期的生产性投资。

data.en: However, lower yields and additional credit may not necessarily support productive investments in the medium to long term when many other factors come into play.
data.zh: 在过去15年的量化宽松期间，主要经济体的固定资本形成增长率与危机前相比有所下降(图十)。

data.en: During the quantitative easing episodes of the past 15 years, growth of fixed capital formation in major economies decreased compared with growth in the pre-crisis period (figure X).
data.zh: 图十

data.en: Figure X
data.zh: 若干发达经济体固定资本形成的增长

data.en: Growth of fixed capital formation in selected developed economies
data.zh: (百分比)

data.en: (Percentage)
data.zh: 资料来源：联合国经济和社会事务部。

data.en: Source: United Nations, Department of Economic and Social Affairs.
data.zh: 注：图中显示的是平均年增长率(使用几何平均数)。

data.en: Over this period, as investors searched for stronger returns and rebalanced their portfolios with riskier assets, additional liquidity flowed into financial sectors and real estate markets, contributing to the pushing up of asset prices.
data.zh: 在实行第一轮量化宽松后的10年里，标准普尔500指数上涨了200%以上，道琼斯欧洲斯托克指数几乎翻了一番。

data.en: In the 10 years after the first round of quantitative easing, the S&P 500 rose by over 200 per cent and the Dow Jones Euro STOXX index nearly doubled.
data.zh: 从疫情期间推出的上一次量化宽松政策到2021年底，标准普尔500指数几乎翻了一番，道琼斯欧洲斯托克指数增加了约50%。

data.en: Between the last quantitative easing introduced during the pandemic and the end of 2021, the S&P 500 nearly doubled and the Dow Jones Euro STOXX index increased by about 50 per cent.
data.zh: 美国和欧洲的房地产价格也出现了类似的走势，更多的流动性追逐现有的住房库存。

data.en: Similar movements were also seen in real estate prices in the United States and Europe, with additional liquidity chasing available housing stocks.
data.zh: 发展中国家：量化宽松的溢出效应

data.en: Quantitative easing boosted global liquidity.
data.zh: 由于发达经济体的长期利率仍然接近于零，投资者寻求更高的收益，导致发展中国家的资产价格上涨。

data.en: As long-term interest rates remained near zero in the developed economies, investors’ search for higher yields contributed to higher asset prices in the developing countries.
data.zh: 许多发展中国家的政府和公司开始在国际资本市场借款。

data.en: Many developing country Governments and corporates began to borrow in the international capital market.
data.zh: 外债的增加和外国证券资本的流入往往导致汇率升值，使进口品更便宜，出口品更昂贵，对许多发展中国家的国际收支产生不利影响。

data.en: In principle, increased global liquidity could lower borrowing costs for some developing countries, especially those with access to international financial markets and strong macroeconomic fundamentals.
data.zh: 然而，虽然这些资金流入有助于在短期内弥补融资缺口，但也导致外债增加。

data.en: However, while such inflows helped to bridge financing gaps in the short run, they also contributed to increases in external debt.
data.zh: 2007年至2020年，政府、企业和家庭的债务总额从占国内生产总值的125%增至243%(图十一)。

data.en: Between 2007 and 2020, total debt of Governments, firms and households increased from 125 per cent of GDP to 243 per cent of GDP (figure XI).
data.zh: 在同一时期，发展中国家的国际债券存量增加了200%以上，达到约7.5万亿美元(图十二)。

data.en: Many developing countries continued to pay high and rising risk premiums during quantitative easing, even though interest rates in the developed economies remained near zero during the decade before the pandemic.
data.zh: 例如，在量化宽松之前的2007年，加纳发行了一笔支付8.5%利息的主权债券，而在2015年，加纳在国际资本市场发行了一笔支付10.75%利息的债券。

data.en: In 2007, for instance, before quantitative easing, Ghana issued a sovereign bond that paid 8.5 per cent interest, while in 2015, it issued a bond in the international capital market that paid 10.75 per cent interest.
data.zh: 撒哈拉以南非洲的其他国家，例如安哥拉、塞内加尔和赞比亚，在量化宽松期间经历了更高的外部借款费用。

data.en: Other countries in sub-Saharan Africa, for example, Angola, Senegal and Zambia, experienced higher external borrowing cost during the period of quantitative easing.
data.zh: 这些国家在危机期间面临甚至更高的借款费用。

data.en: Those countries faced even higher borrowing costs during crises.
data.zh: 例如，赞比亚主权债券收益率在2007年至2019年期间保持在14%至28%之间，在疫情期间飙升至约35%。

data.en: For instance, the yield on the sovereign bonds of Zambia remained between 14 and 28 per cent between 2007 and 2019 and surged to about 35 per cent during the pandemic.
data.zh: 借款费用居高不下加剧了许多发展中国家的债务可持续性风险。

data.en: Persistently high borrowing costs have exacerbated the debt sustainability risks of many developing countries.
data.zh: 截至2023年2月，在低收入国家债务可持续性框架涵盖的69个国家中，有36个国家陷入债务困境或面临债务困境的高风险。

data.en: As of February 2023, 36 out of 69 countries covered by the Debt Sustainability Framework for Low-Income Countries were in debt distress or at high risk of experiencing debt distress.
data.zh: 图十一
